opp.name: AdaptHealth [S2P] April'26
opp.next_step: Discussion Summary: Current environment: large, distributed U.S. footprint with a small indirect team, no formal Source-to-Pay platform in place, and a desire to become more strategic in sourcing. Overview of Zycus’ unified S2P suite and agentic AI capabilities (Merlin Intake via MS Teams, autonomous tail-spend negotiations, supplier onboarding and risk, touchless invoicing, and embedded analytics). How our platform is used by organizations of all sizes to improve user adoption, reduce cycle times, and unlock strategic capacity for procurement. Next Steps: Edward (Zycus): Send you today’s slide deck so you can review and socialize internally. Bryan (AdaptHealth): Share the deck with your team and gather feedback on interest and potential fit across the broader business. Chris (Zycus): Follow up with you in a couple of weeks to see what feedback you’ve received and, if helpful, align on a deeper demo and/or a pro bono ROI assessment to support any future business case.
opp.ais_why:
• Bryan Rottkamp sat through a full agentic S2P pitch in April 2026 covering Merlin Intake on Teams, Anna autonomous tail-spend negotiation, and touchless invoicing. He asked pointed questions like 'agentic AI finding outcomes' and how the AP automation negotiates.
• AdaptHealth has no formal Source-to-Pay today across 700+ locations, so the agentic vision lands cleanly. SF notes mention 'agentic AI capabilities' and 'autonomous tail-spend negotiations' as the headline pitch.
• Bryan is taking the deck to socialize internally and Edward will follow up in two weeks for a pro-bono ROI assessment. Real engagement, but no champion or budget yet - upper-curious range.

champion.narrative: Bryan Rottkamp is the sole engaged contact, positioned as a potential developing champion. He participated in the discovery call, expressed interest in Zycus's capabilities, and committed to 'share the deck with his team and gather feedback on interest and potential fit across the broader business.' However, his Manager-level role limits his ability to drive enterprise procurement transformation across 700+ locations without executive air cover. There is no evidence of advocacy actions (introducing Zycus to senior stakeholders, pushing back on competing priorities, accelerating the evaluation timeline) in the 71 days since the call. Bryan has not responded to Zycus's follow-up outreach (LastActivityDate 2026-06-24 indicates rep-initiated activity, but no buyer reply captured in Next_Step__c or tasks). His engagement has cooled from active (discovery call) to passive (no follow-up meeting scheduled). Without access to the Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or procurement leadership, Bryan cannot champion this deal to close. He is a necessary coach and internal navigator, but insufficient as the sole relationship. Champion status: developing, currently passive, at risk of further cooling without re-engagement.

competition.narrative: No competitors have been identified or discussed. The discovery call positioned AdaptHealth as a greenfield opportunity with 'no formal Source-to-Pay platform in place,' suggesting sole-sourced or uncontested evaluation. However, this assumption is unvalidated. Salesforce fields (Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c) are empty or unread, and no competitive intelligence appeared in Next_Step__c, Next_Step_History__c, or task descriptions. For a $110k enterprise S2P deal, it is plausible AdaptHealth will benchmark Zycus against Coupa, SAP Ariba, Jaggaer, or GEP, or that they are conducting quiet diligence before issuing an RFP. The lack of competitive visibility is a risk: if AdaptHealth is multi-tracking and Zycus is unaware, differentiation and champion-building are reactive rather than proactive. The recommended move is to surface competitive landscape explicitly in the follow-up conversation (e.g., 'Are you evaluating other platforms alongside us, or is this a sole-source exploration?') and position Zycus's Merlin agentic AI and unified suite as differentiators if competition emerges.

identify_pain.narrative: Bryan Rottkamp articulated operational pain on the discovery call: AdaptHealth has 'a large, distributed U.S. footprint with a small indirect team, no formal Source-to-Pay platform in place, and a desire to become more strategic in sourcing.' This indicates manual, decentralized procurement processes with limited visibility, low strategic leverage, and reliance on a lean indirect procurement team to manage spend across 700+ locations. The pain is owned by Bryan (Manager-level, likely Category Manager for indirect spend) but the enterprise-wide impact suggests CFO/finance and operations leadership also feel the inefficiency. The absence of a formal S2P platform means AdaptHealth likely suffers from maverick spend, slow cycle times, supplier fragmentation, and limited spend analytics. However, the pain has not been quantified (no cost of manual processing, no savings opportunity baseline, no FTE burden stated), and no compelling event or deadline was mentioned to force urgency. The pro-bono ROI assessment is the planned vehicle to translate qualitative pain into quantified business case.
